title: Create and provision companies
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want to create and provision new tenant companies on the platform, so that subscribing organizations can begin using the HRMS.
acceptance_criteria: Given I hold the HRMS Administrator role, when I initiate creation of a new company, then a new tenant company record is created and provisioned on the platform.; Given a company is being provisioned, when provisioning runs, then a company-specific isolated data space is established for its organizational structures, policies, security, workflows, and workforce data.; Given a company is provisioned, when provisioning completes, then the company becomes available for further configuration and onboarding.; Given I do not hold the HRMS Administrator role, when I attempt to create a company, then the action is denied.

title: Maintain business and legal details
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to record and maintain my company's business and legal details, so that the platform holds accurate identifying, legal, and contact information.
acceptance_criteria: Given a company record, when I edit its profile, then I can capture legal name, trade name, and website.; Given a company record, when I edit its profile, then I can capture registration identifiers including GSTN and EIN.; Given a company record, when I edit its profile, then I can capture contact information.; Given required business and legal fields are provided, when I save, then the details persist against the company; Given a field is missing or malformed, when I save, then a validation error is shown and the record is not saved.; Given business or legal details are updated later, when I save changes, then the updated values are retained against the company.

title: Operate across multiple jurisdictions
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want my company to operate across one or more jurisdictions, so that we can run operations wherever we do business under a single tenant.
acceptance_criteria: Given a company, when I configure jurisdictions, then the company can be associated with one or more jurisdictions.; Given multiple jurisdictions are configured, when the company operates, then each jurisdiction is supported without requiring a separate tenant.; Given a company operates in a jurisdiction, when I configure it, then jurisdiction-specific organizational structures, policies, security, workflows, and workforce data can be applied per jurisdiction.

title: Company-specific configuration
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want company-specific configuration of organizational structures, policies, security, workflows, and workforce data, so that our setup reflects our own operating model.
acceptance_criteria: Given a company, when I configure it, then organizational structures, policies, security settings, workflows, and workforce data can each be set specifically for that company.; Given company-specific configuration exists, when another company is configured, then its settings remain independent and do not affect mine.; Given I am scoped to my company, when I change configuration, then the change applies only within my company's isolated data space.

title: Company-level data isolation
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want the system to preserve data isolation at the company level, so that each tenant's data remains private and secure from other tenants.
acceptance_criteria: Given multiple companies exist on the platform, when data is stored or accessed, then each company's data is isolated from every other company.; Given a user is scoped to one company, when they access data, then they cannot access another company's data.; Given a company operates as standalone, within a group, or under a shared services provider, when data is accessed, then company-level isolation is enforced regardless of the operating model.

title: Support standalone, group, and shared-services operating models
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want to support companies operating as standalone, within a group-company structure, or under an external shared services provider, so that the platform fits diverse ownership and management arrangements.
acceptance_criteria: Given a company, when I set its operating model, then it can be designated as an independent standalone company.; Given a company, when I set its operating model, then it can be designated as part of a group-company structure.; Given a company, when I set its operating model, then it can be designated as managed by an external shared services provider.; Given any of these models is selected, when data is accessed, then company-level data isolation is still preserved.; Given an operating model is set, when it changes later, then isolation and configuration continue to apply correctly under the new model.

title: Commercial packaging and subscription models
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want commercial packaging and subscription models based on number of companies, number of employees, and subscribed modules, so that customers can be billed according to their usage and entitlements.
acceptance_criteria: Given a subscription, when it is configured, then packaging can be based on number of companies.; Given a subscription, when it is configured, then packaging can be based on number of employees.; Given a subscription, when it is configured, then packaging can be based on the set of subscribed modules.; Given a subscription is defined, when a customer subscribes, then the applicable package and limits are applied.; Given multiple packaging dimensions are combined, when the subscription is priced, then company count, employee count, and module selection are all reflected.

title: Company inactivation and data retention on closure
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want to mark companies inactive upon closure and retain their data per policy, so that closed or merged companies are handled compliantly.
acceptance_criteria: Given a company closes, when I process the closure, then the company is marked inactive.; Given a company is closed or merged, when it is archived, then its data is preserved for 7 years subject to archival fees.; Given a company is inactive, when the retention period applies, then data retention follows the defined policy.; Given a company is marked inactive, when users attempt normal operations, then active operations are disabled while retained data remains available for retention purposes.

title: Export entire company data on platform exit
user_story: As a Company Admin of a customer leaving the platform, I want to export the entire company data, so that we retain our records when we discontinue the service.
acceptance_criteria: Given a customer is leaving the platform, when they request an export, then the entire company data can be exported.; Given an export is generated, when it completes, then it contains the company's full dataset for handover.; Given the export is scoped to my company, when it is produced, then it includes only my company's data and no other tenant's data.

title: Manage companies within a group-company structure
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want to manage the set of companies that belong to my group-company structure, so that related entities are administered together while remaining isolated.
acceptance_criteria: Given a group-company structure exists, when I view the group, then I can see and administer the companies that belong to the group.; Given a company is part of a group, when I configure it, then group-level oversight is possible while each member company's data remains isolated at the company level.; Given I am a Group Company Admin, when I attempt to access a company outside my group, then the action is denied.

title: Manage companies as an external shared services provider
user_story: As a Portfolio Admin acting as an external shared services provider, I want to manage the companies assigned to me, so that I can administer multiple client tenants on their behalf.
acceptance_criteria: Given a company is designated as managed by an external shared services provider, when I am assigned as its administrator, then I can administer that company's configuration and data.; Given I manage multiple client companies, when I access each one, then company-level data isolation is preserved between clients.; Given I am not assigned to a company, when I attempt to access it, then the action is denied.

title: Enforce subscription entitlements and limits
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want the platform to enforce the limits of each subscription package, so that usage stays within the customer's entitlements for companies, employees, and modules.
acceptance_criteria: Given a subscription defines a company limit, when the limit would be exceeded, then further company creation is prevented or flagged.; Given a subscription defines an employee limit, when the limit would be exceeded, then the excess is prevented or flagged per policy.; Given a subscription defines a set of subscribed modules, when a user accesses a module, then access is granted only for subscribed modules.; Given entitlements change, when the subscription is updated, then the applied limits and module access are updated accordingly.

title: Apply archival fees for retained closed-company data
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want to apply archival fees for the data of closed or merged companies retained for 7 years, so that long-term retention is billed according to policy.
acceptance_criteria: Given a closed or merged company's data is archived, when it is retained, then it is preserved for 7 years subject to archival fees.; Given archival fees apply, when the retention period is active, then the fees are tracked and billable to the customer.; Given the 7-year retention period elapses, when retention ends, then the data is handled per the defined disposal or retention policy.

title: Preserve and access data for closed or merged companies
user_story: As a Company Admin of a closed or merged company, I want our data preserved and accessible per the retention policy, so that we can meet compliance and record-keeping obligations after closure.
acceptance_criteria: Given my company is marked inactive on closure, when the retention policy applies, then our data is preserved for 7 years.; Given my company was merged into another, when data is archived, then the merged company's records remain preserved per policy.; Given retained data exists, when I need our records, then I can request an export of the entire company data for handover.

title: Enforce uniqueness and integrity of company registration identifiers
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want registration identifiers and legal identity validated for uniqueness and integrity at the data layer, so that no two companies collide on regulated identifiers and records stay trustworthy.
acceptance_criteria: Given company registration identifiers such as GSTN and EIN, when a company record is created or updated, then the platform enforces uniqueness and format-integrity constraints on those identifiers at the data layer.; Given a duplicate GSTN or EIN, when I attempt to save, then the record is rejected with an integrity error.; Given legal name and trade name, when a record is persisted, then not-null and referential integrity constraints are enforced so the canonical company entity remains valid.

title: Maintain bitemporal history of company profile and lifecycle status
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want changes to my company's profile and lifecycle status to be effective-dated and historically preserved, so that we can see what was true at any point in time.
acceptance_criteria: Given a change to business, legal, or contact details, when I save it with an effective date, then the prior values are retained as history and the new values apply from the effective date.; Given a lifecycle status change such as active, inactive, or merged, when it is recorded, then the change is effective-dated and previous states remain queryable.; Given a point in time, when I query the company record, then the platform returns the values that were effective and recorded as of that time.

title: Model group-company relationships in the data model
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want the parent-subsidiary relationships between companies in my group represented in the canonical data model, so that group structure is queryable while each company stays isolated.
acceptance_criteria: Given companies belonging to a group, when the group structure is defined, then parent-subsidiary relationships are stored as first-class relationships in the data model.; Given a group relationship, when it is persisted, then referential integrity links member companies to the group without collapsing their company-level data isolation.; Given the group changes over time, when membership is added or removed, then the relationship changes are effective-dated and preserved.

title: Toggle subscribed modules via governed configuration
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want subscribed modules represented as per-tenant governed configuration, so that enabling or disabling modules changes system behavior without code.
acceptance_criteria: Given my company's subscription, when subscribed modules are set, then each module toggle is stored as versioned, effective-dated per-tenant configuration.; Given a module toggle changes, when it is saved, then the change is applied without a code deployment and the prior configuration version is retained.; Given the module configuration, when the engines read it at runtime, then only toggled-on modules are treated as enabled for my company.

title: Define subscription packages as governed versioned config
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want subscription packages and their limits defined as governed configuration, so that packaging by companies, employees, and modules can be changed and versioned without code.
acceptance_criteria: Given commercial packaging, when I define a subscription package, then its company-count, employee-count, and module-set limits are stored as versioned, effective-dated configuration.; Given a package definition changes, when I save a new version, then the change is effective-dated and prior versions remain auditable.; Given a customer subscribes, when the package is applied, then the engines read the current package configuration to determine entitlements.

title: Rules engine evaluates subscription entitlements from config
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want the shared Rules engine to evaluate subscription entitlements from configured package limits, so that entitlement decisions are consistent and driven by config rather than hard-coded logic.
acceptance_criteria: Given configured package limits, when a company or employee is created or a module is accessed, then the Rules engine evaluates the request against the package decision table.; Given the evaluation, when a limit would be exceeded, then the engine returns a deny or flag decision that the platform enforces.; Given package configuration changes, when the engine next evaluates, then it uses the updated limits without any code change.

title: Company directory with search filter and navigation
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a company directory screen with search, filter, and navigation, so that I can quickly find and open the companies I administer.
acceptance_criteria: Given companies I am entitled to see, when I open the company directory, then they are presented in a metadata-driven grid scoped to my access.; Given the directory, when I search or filter by attributes such as legal name, trade name, jurisdiction, or status, then the grid updates to matching companies only.; Given a company row, when I select it, then I navigate to that company's detail screen; Given I lack access to a company, when I search, then it does not appear in my results.

title: Metadata-driven company profile screen
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a metadata-driven company profile screen to view and edit business, legal, jurisdiction, and configuration details, so that I can self-serve company setup through the SPA.
acceptance_criteria: Given a company, when I open its profile screen, then business, legal, contact, jurisdiction, and configuration sections render from metadata-driven forms.; Given I edit a field, when validation rules from configuration apply, then inline validation feedback is shown before save.; Given I save valid changes, when the form submits via the API/BFF, then the changes persist and the screen reflects the updated values.; Given fields I am not permitted to edit, when the screen renders, then those fields are read-only or hidden per my role.
